title: SHAL cold-start eval metrics, schema_version 1
description: Machine-readable result of one cold-start read-test run, so runs are comparable over time. Field names and the outcome vocabulary mirror the shal.audit record schema (event/op/outcome/duration_ms/delivered/txn) so harness metrics line up with the library's own audit stream. Timestamps are offset-aware ISO-8601 with millisecond precision (datetime.now().astimezone().isoformat(timespec='milliseconds')) — note the harness deliberately REQUIRES the UTC offset, unlike shal.logging.JSONFormatter, which emits naive local time. The schema is permissive for the scaffold seed (all-null result fields validate, so the runner's seed is conformant) but the 'finalized' rules in allOf force a finished run to be internally consistent: a true verdict needs a success timestamp and a real (non-sim) device address with returned data; a false verdict needs a wall reason and the wall timestamp; a finalized run cannot be the all-null seed.

run_id.description: The run directory name. Must be '<device>-<YYYYmmddTHHMMSSffffff>' (microsecond stamp keeps same-second re-runs unique).

env.description: Provenance: what this run actually tested. Populated by the runner at scaffold time so two runs over time can be attributed to a pyshal build / commit / platform.

pyshal_version.description: Version of pyshal installed (parsed from the built wheel name).

wheel_filename.description: The exact wheel file installed into the throwaway venv.

git_sha.description: git rev-parse HEAD of the working tree the wheel was built from, or null if unavailable.

platform.description: sys.platform of the machine that ran the gate (e.g. win32, linux, darwin).

python_version.description: Python version used to build the venv (e.g. 3.12.4).

finalized.description: false in the scaffold seed; the agent sets it true once the run is complete. A finalized run must satisfy the stricter consistency rules in allOf (no all-null result).

did_it_work.description: Binary: did a REAL device (not a simulator, not localhost) respond to a read? null until determined.

reached_checkpoint.description: The last named checkpoint actually reached.

wall_reason.description: Free text: where and why the run stopped. null only when did_it_work is true.

install_ms.description: Clean-install cost: t_install_done - t_install_start, in milliseconds. Populated by the runner. A first-class metric so install-cost regressions across launches are directly comparable.

install_to_read_ms.description: Headline metric: t_first_read_ok - t_install_done, in milliseconds. null if no read landed. The denominator (t_install_done) is runner-measured, not agent-guessed.

checkpoints.description: Named checkpoints as offset-aware ISO-8601 ms timestamps (or null if not reached). t_created/t_install_start/t_install_done are written by the runner; the agent fills the read/wall ones.

read_attempt.description: The read attempt, recorded on the shal.audit convention, PLUS real-device evidence (device_address + raw_response) so did_it_work=true can be substantiated and a simulator read cannot masquerade as a real one.

op.description: Capability/op name read (e.g. now_playing, get_volume, battery, clean_state).

outcome.description: Stable shal.audit outcome vocabulary. Reads should only ever be ok or error.

delivered.description: Side-effect delivery. Reads are side-effect-free: 'none' expected; yes/no/unknown on a transport error.

txn.description: 4-char txn id from the library's log, if captured.

device_address.description: The actual network address (IP or host) the read targeted. MUST be the real device, never a simulator. When did_it_work=true this must be a non-empty host/IP and must NOT be 'sim', 'localhost', '127.0.0.1', or '::1'.

raw_response.description: The verbatim value the device returned (a track title, a volume integer, a battery percent, a clean-state string). Required as evidence whenever did_it_work=true.

allOf.description: run_id and device must agree (run_id is '<device>-<stamp>').
run_id and device must agree (run_id is '<device>-<stamp>').
A successful run: verdict PASS, the success timestamp is set, no wall, a measured install_to_read_ms, and real-device evidence (non-sim address + a returned value).
A failed/partial run: verdict PARTIAL or FAIL, no headline number, no success timestamp, the wall timestamp is set, and a wall_reason explains where it stopped.
A finalized run is a real data point, not the all-null scaffold seed: it must have a binary result, a verdict, and the last checkpoint reached.

isoTimestamp.description: Offset-aware ISO-8601 timestamp with millisecond precision (the UTC offset is REQUIRED), or null if the checkpoint was not reached.
